facts.text: Boston was founded in 1630 on Shawmut Peninsula by English Puritan settlers who named it after Boston, Lincolnshire.
The first free public library in the United States, the Boston Public Library, opened its doors in 1852.
America’s first public school, Boston Latin School, was founded in 1635, just five years after the city itself.
Back Bay was formed by leveling Boston’s original hills and importing gravel from Needham, and today it houses landmarks like the Public Library and Trinity Church.
King’s Chapel, Boston’s first Anglican church founded in 1686, became a Unitarian church in 1785, reflecting the city’s religious evolution.
The Boston Celtics, founded in 1946, have won eighteen NBA championships, the most of any team in league history.
